items.title: Ep17. Why do different countries speak different languages?
items.desc: In this episode, Arthur asks why people in different countries speak different languages. Lily and Max take a fun world tour through mountains, accents, writing systems, travelling words, baby language detectives, and why words can carry family, culture, history and home. Along the way, they discover that languages are not just different sounds, they are also people’s stories.

items.title: Ep16. Why can't we have rainbow skin?
items.desc: In this colourful episode, Lily and Max explore Zelda’s brilliant question: why can’t we have rainbow skin? They discover what gives human skin its colour, how rainbows are made from light, and the surprising hidden patterns called Blaschko’s lines that most people can’t see.

items.title: Ep15. Why some people have straight hair and others have curly hair?
items.desc: In this episode of Little Ears, Big Ideas, Lily and Max explore why some people have straight hair while others have curls, waves, or coils. With help from Alex’s question, they discover tiny hair follicles, genes, keratin, frizz, and why every hair type is wonderfully different.

items.title: Ep14. Why do kids’ teeth fall out?
items.desc: In this episode of Little Ears, Big Ideas, Lily and Max investigate the wobbly mystery of baby teeth! Why do teeth fall out, what is hiding underneath, and why do grown-up teeth take their place? With silly tooth jokes, gentle science, and a very proud gap smile, this episode helps kids understand one of the biggest little milestones of growing up.

items.title: Ep13. Why do spiders make webs?
items.desc: In this episode, Lily and Max explore why spiders make webs . They discover that spider webs are not just spooky decorations. They are clever tools for catching food, building shelters, protecting eggs, and even making safety lines. This episode turns spider science into a fun, friendly adventure for curious kids.
